message:
test(e2e): add OpenGraph metadata and image E2E tests

- Add og-metadata.spec.ts testing OG/Twitter tags on all pages
- Add og-images.spec.ts testing OG image endpoint responses

39 tests covering:
- Homepage, prompts, bundles, workflows, user profiles OG tags
- og:title, og:description, og:type, og:url, og:image presence
- Twitter Card (summary_large_image) tags
- OG image endpoints returning PNG with correct content-type
- Fallback handling for missing content
- Cache headers on image responses
- Dynamic OG images per content type

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -316,7 +316,7 @@ {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-t4lf","title":"Set up i18n framework for multi-language support","description":"## Origin Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-xhrb. Keep both repos in sync. ## Task Set up internationalization framework for future multi-language support. ## Why This Matters - Global market expansion - Non-English users - Better accessibility - Future-proofing ## Implementation ### 1. i18n Library Setup Using next-intl for Next.js App Router: ```typescript // next.config.js const withNextIntl = require('next-intl/plugin')('./src/i18n.ts'); module.exports = withNextIntl({ // other config }); ``` ### 2. Message Files Structure ``` messages/ ├── en.json # English (default) ├── es.json # Spanish ├── fr.json # French ├── de.json # German ├── ja.json # Japanese └── zh.json # Chinese ``` ### 3. Message Format ```json { "common": { "save": "Save", "cancel": "Cancel", "delete": "Delete", "loading": "Loading..." }, "auth": { "signIn": "Sign In", "signOut": "Sign Out", "signUp": "Create Account" }, "prompts": { "title": "My Prompts", "create": "Create Prompt", "empty": "You don't have any prompts yet." }, "errors": { "generic": "Something went wrong. Please try again.", "notFound": "Page not found", "unauthorized": "Please sign in to continue" } } ``` ### 4. Usage in Components ```tsx import { useTranslations } from 'next-intl'; function PromptList() { const t = useTranslations('prompts'); return (

{t('title')}

{prompts.length === 0 &&

{t('empty')}

}
); } ``` ### 5. Server Components ```tsx import { getTranslations } from 'next-intl/server'; async function Page() { const t = await getTranslations('prompts'); return

{t('title')}

; } ``` ### 6. URL Structure ``` /en/dashboard # English /es/dashboard # Spanish /fr/dashboard # French ``` ### 7. Language Detection - Browser preference - User setting (stored) - URL path prefix - Default fallback: English ## Database Changes ```typescript // Add to user_profiles preferredLanguage: text('preferred_language').default('en'), ``` ## Initial Languages - English (en) - Default, complete - Spanish (es) - High priority - French (fr) - High priority - German (de) - Medium priority - Japanese (ja) - Medium priority - Chinese (zh) - Medium priority ## Implementation Phases 1. **Phase 1**: Framework setup, extract all strings (this bead) 2. **Phase 2**: Professional translation 3. **Phase 3**: RTL support (Arabic, Hebrew) ## Files to Create - src/i18n.ts - src/middleware.ts (locale handling) - messages/en.json - src/components/language-switcher.tsx ## Success Criteria - Framework configured - All UI strings extracted - Language switching works - Default English complete ","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:13.986010471-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:13.986010471-05:00"} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-t524","title":"Implement API documentation for CLI and integrations","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-81ql. ## Task Create comprehensive API documentation for CLI users and potential integrations. ## Why This Matters - CLI users need API reference - Future integration possibilities - Developer experience - Self-service troubleshooting ## Documentation Sections ### 1. Authentication - OAuth flow for CLI - Device code flow - Token management - API key usage (if applicable) ### 2. Endpoints Reference For each endpoint: - Method and URL - Request parameters - Request body schema - Response schema - Error codes - Rate limits - Example requests/responses ### 3. Common Patterns - Pagination - Filtering - Sorting - Error handling - Rate limiting ### 4. Guides - Quick start - Authentication setup - Common use cases - Best practices ## Implementation ### OpenAPI Spec ```yaml openapi: 3.0.3 info: title: JeffreysPrompts API version: 1.0.0 paths: /api/prompts: get: summary: List prompts parameters: - name: limit in: query schema: type: integer default: 20 responses: '200': description: List of prompts content: application/json: schema: $ref: '#/components/schemas/PromptList' ``` ### Auto-Generation - Generate from Zod schemas - Keep in sync with implementation - Version the API ### Documentation Site - /docs/api - Main docs page - Interactive API explorer - Code examples (curl, JS, Python) - Searchable ## Files to Create - src/app/(public)/docs/api/page.tsx - src/app/(public)/docs/api/[...slug]/page.tsx - content/docs/api/*.mdx - src/lib/api/openapi.ts - public/openapi.json ## Success Criteria - All endpoints documented - Examples are accurate - Kept in sync with code - Easy to navigate ## Cross-Repo Integration - Premium CLI endpoints to document: jeffreysprompts_premium-67o - Premium device code auth endpoints: jeffreysprompts_premium-0o1 - Premium token refresh/revoke endpoints: jeffreysprompts_premium-0ve - Premium CLI JWT/token details: {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-tco","title":"Set up CLI configuration system (lib/config.ts)","description":"**Details:** - Configuration file at $HOME/.config/jfp/config.json - Supports: registry URL, cache settings, skills directories, preferences - loadConfig() reads with defaults - saveConfig() persists changes - Environment variable overrides **Config Schema:** ```typescript interface JfpConfig { registry: { url: string; // default: https://jeffreysprompts.com cacheTtl: number; // default: 3600 (1 hour) autoRefresh: boolean; // default: true }; skills: { personalDir: string; // default: $HOME/.config/claude/skills projectDir: string; // default: .claude/skills preferProject: boolean; // default: false }; output: { color: boolean; // default: true (TTY detection) json: boolean; // default: false }; updates: { autoCheck: boolean; // default: true autoUpdate: boolean; // default: false (opt-in) }; } ``` **Implementation:** ```typescript // packages/cli/src/lib/config.ts const CONFIG_PATH = join(homedir(), '.config', 'jfp', 'config.json'); const DEFAULT_CONFIG: JfpConfig = { registry: { url: 'https://jeffreysprompts.com', cacheTtl: 3600, autoRefresh: true, }, skills: { personalDir: join(homedir(), '.config', 'claude', 'skills'), projectDir: '.claude/skills', preferProject: false, }, output: { color: true, json: false, }, updates: { autoCheck: true, autoUpdate: false, }, }; export function loadConfig(): JfpConfig { try { const raw = readFileSync(CONFIG_PATH, 'utf-8'); return { ...DEFAULT_CONFIG, ...JSON.parse(raw) }; } catch { return DEFAULT_CONFIG; } } export function saveConfig(config: Partial): void { const current = loadConfig(); const merged = { ...current, ...config }; mkdirSync(dirname(CONFIG_PATH), { recursive: true }); writeFileSync(CONFIG_PATH, JSON.stringify(merged, null, 2)); } ``` **Environment Overrides:** - JFP_REGISTRY_URL - JFP_CACHE_TTL - JFP_NO_COLOR **Location:** packages/cli/src/lib/config.ts **Reasoning:** Configuration system enables customization without modifying code. Environment overrides support CI/CD pipelines and containerized usage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:36:07.910360593-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T01:31:30.494123526-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T01:31:30.494123526-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented config defaults + env overrides","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-tco","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-xdt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:37:27.593970446-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-tdap","title":"Task: E2E test - Homepage load and prompt grid display","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:46:31.212249072-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T19:02:39.424195068-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T19:02:39.424195068-05:00","close_reason":"Created comprehensive homepage E2E test suite with 17 tests covering page load, hero section, prompt grid, filters, footer, responsive layout, and performance","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-tdap","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-945","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:56:34.312421861-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-tf47","title":"Implement OpenGraph and social sharing previews","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-tvyv. ## Task Add OpenGraph and Twitter Card meta tags for rich social sharing. ## Why This Matters - Shared links look professional - Increases click-through rates - Viral potential - SEO benefits ## Pages with Social Previews ### Public Pages - Landing page - Public prompts - Public collections - User profiles - Shared prompt links ### Dynamic OG Images - Generate images for prompts - Include title, description, author - Branded template - Cache for performance ## Implementation ### Metadata API ```typescript // src/app/(public)/prompts/[id]/page.tsx export async function generateMetadata({ params }): Promise { const prompt = await getPrompt(params.id); return { title: prompt.title, description: prompt.description, openGraph: { title: prompt.title, description: prompt.description, type: 'article', url: `https://jeffreysprompts.com/prompts/${prompt.id}`, images: [{ url: `https://jeffreysprompts.com/api/og/prompt/${prompt.id}`, width: 1200, height: 630, alt: prompt.title, }], authors: [prompt.author.name], }, twitter: { card: 'summary_large_image', title: prompt.title, description: prompt.description, images: [`https://jeffreysprompts.com/api/og/prompt/${prompt.id}`], }, }; } ``` ### OG Image Generation ```typescript // src/app/api/og/prompt/[id]/route.tsx import { ImageResponse } from 'next/og'; export async function GET(request: Request, { params }) { const prompt = await getPrompt(params.id); return new ImageResponse( (
{prompt.title}
{prompt.description?.slice(0, 150)}
{prompt.author.name}
), { width: 1200, height: 630 } ); } ``` ### Templates - Prompt OG template - Collection OG template - Profile OG template - Generic fallback ## Testing - Facebook Sharing Debugger - Twitter Card Validator - LinkedIn Post Inspector - Preview in Slack/Discord ## Files to Create - src/app/api/og/prompt/[id]/route.tsx - src/app/api/og/collection/[id]/route.tsx - src/app/api/og/profile/[id]/route.tsx - src/lib/og/templates.tsx ## Success Criteria - All public pages have OG tags - Dynamic images generate correctly - Images cached for performance - Previews look professional ## Test Coverage - Integration: jeffreysprompts_premium-9vg3 (OG metadata + images) - E2E: jeffreysprompts_premium-xua includes OG tag checks ## UX Additions - Cache OG image responses with immutable caching headers - Provide fallback avatar and title when author data missing - Limit text length to avoid overflow in OG layout ","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:23:40.635814722-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T12:54:54.595962228-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T12:54:54.595962228-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented OG images for all public pages: prompts, bundles, workflows, user profiles. Added metadata with openGraph and twitter card properties.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-tf47","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-30k9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:35.674848646-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-tf47","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-2uex","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:35.808168364-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
